personas.name: F&B Chain Ops Director
personas.icp: Ops director / multi-outlet manager at an SG F&B chain (8–20 outlets, ~120–200 shift staff across kitchen + front-of-house) fighting daily no-shows on PWM-anchored hourly wages of S$12.09+, currently running an illegal employer WhatsApp 'name and shame' group of 30–60 ops peers.
pains: Daily no-shows force backfill at 1.5x overtime premium plus lost covers — S$211–450 per unfilled shift
F&B PWM wage trajectory (S$12.09 → S$13.45/hr by 2027) compounds every no-show cost ~11% over the contract horizon
Reference checks via WhatsApp groups expose the chain to PDPA + TAFEP risk and cannot be cited in a TGFEP complaint
Aon SG 2024 — S$3.3B sector-wide absenteeism cost but no shared early-warning system across the 15,700 F&B establishments
Hiring cycle is sub-48-hr: a 5–15 day Sterling/HireRight check is unusable for shift roster gaps
personas.current_workaround: Closed WhatsApp / Telegram group with 30–60 F&B ops peers (free, instant, legally fragile per PDPA Section 17); supplemented by 30–60 minute phone calls to past employers that >60% of the time return boilerplate or silence (HRSingapore community evidence); manager keeps a personal Notes-app 'lemon list' that doesn't travel between outlets.

nba.summary: Lemon-loss + WhatsApp ref-check time: 15 outlets × 10 shift staff × 2.5% lemon rate × ~40 incidents/yr × S$420 / 12 + 12 hr/wk ref-check time at S$50/hr. Buyer's perceived software budget is structurally ~5% of displaced labour cost (SG F&B SME norm) — gap is commercial, not measurement; PSG funding closes it to a 'half-of-Confluence' feel.

implications.headline: F&B chain ops is the primary-value persona for Y1
implications.body: 9,400 reachable F&B SMEs × S$2,400 avg annual spend = S$23M SAM stack — the largest single SAM contributor. Agent 4 should design the wedge attack plan around this persona; Agent 8 must show net price after PSG first on the F&B-facing pricing card.

personas.name: Licensed Security Agency Manager
personas.icp: Operations manager at a PLRD-licensed SG security agency (50–500 officers deployed across condo / commercial / event sites) carrying MOM-regulated headcount obligations, AOG-style client penalties of S$200–500 per missed deployment, and a CEI/AEAS-trained recruitment team currently relying on phone-call references that 60%+ of the time return silence.
pains: Client SLAs impose S$200–500 penalties per missed officer deployment — a 3% lemon roster on 200 officers (~6 lemons) drives 6+ AOG events/month
PLRD licence renewal demands documented vetting + disciplinary trail; current phone-call refs cannot be audited
MAS-style 'rolling bad apples' regime is on the horizon for PLRD (per A&O Shearman 2024) — agencies want to be ahead of the rule
CEI/AEAS-certified recruiters cost S$3,500–5,500/mo loaded and burn 15+ hr/wk on reference chasing
Workforce concentration: surviving SG agency count fell from ~3,900 (2016) to 1,600–1,800 (2025) — larger surviving agencies are more sophisticated but margin-squeezed by S$2.5k–3.5k officer wages
personas.current_workaround: Phone calls to last 2–3 employers (~60% no-reply rate per HRSingapore threads) + handwritten 'do not redeploy' list at branch level + PLRD officer-licence number lookup (which only confirms the licence is current, not the officer's reliability); some agencies share informal blocklists via the Security Association of Singapore (SAS) member chat.

nba.summary: 200 officers × 3% lemon rate × 40 incidents/yr × S$650 avg loss (SLA penalty + replacement) ÷ 12 + 15 hr/wk recruiter ref-chasing at S$45/hr. WTP-vs-NBA gap is the SG SME software-budget norm (~4%); WFA-Ready tier at S$1,250/mo closes the gap toward the 0.08–0.12 zone where compliance-tool buyers actually transact.

implications.headline: Security agency tier can sustain S$599+/mo list price
implications.body: MAS-style regime extension to PLRD is on a 12–24 month horizon; agencies will pay enterprise-band pricing for compliance-tracked refs. Tier 4 (WFA-Ready) should target this persona alongside large F&B chains.

personas.name: MICE / Events Staffing Planner
personas.icp: Production / staffing lead at an SG MICE / event agency (SACEOS or AEO-SG member) burst-hiring 50–200 freelance crew per weekend for trade shows, F1, Art Week, conferences — where a single no-show on event day cannot be backfilled and triggers S$800–2,000 in immediate client recovery cost.
pains: Burst hiring window of 7–14 days before event makes traditional reference checks impossible
Single no-show on event day = unbackfillable (the show has already started) — direct client-facing failure
Freelance crew pool of ~30K MICE workers cycles across competitor agencies; no shared reliability signal
STB MICE market USD 4.8B (2025) is growing — but margin squeeze on staffing means a 3% lemon rate destroys event profitability
Reputation damage from a single bad crew member follows the agency to the next RFP
personas.current_workaround: Internal Airtable / Notion 'preferred crew' list shared via Slack; SACEOS / AEO-SG industry word-of-mouth; LinkedIn DM to ex-managers (slow, low hit rate); booking same crew repeatedly which inflates wages and concentrates risk.

nba.summary: 4 events/quarter × 100 burst-hires × 3% lemon × S$800 avg loss × 8 lifecycle incidents per event ÷ 12 — annualised across slow + peak weeks. Per-lookup buying behaviour means buyers pay per-burst-week rather than smoothly — 'expected' here is the soft monthly commit; per-lookup overage is the real revenue.

personas.name: Dormitory / Construction Agency Principal
personas.icp: Principal / GM of an SG construction-side staffing agency or dormitory operator (200–1,000 work-permit holders across BCA-registered sites) carrying recurring contract terms with main contractors who penalise AWOL/runaway events at S$1,000+ in lost productivity + repatriation + re-recruitment.
pains: AWOL / runaway events cost S$1,072 per incident (mid of the brief's S$211–1,072 range) — and recur at ~1.5% annually across the worker roster
Defection-to-rival agencies is endemic in the work-permit ecosystem; no shared signal across agencies on which workers serial-defect
BCA + MOM dormitory regime requires documented worker conduct trails post-Westlite + Tuas dormitory cases
Post-PWA 2024 platform-worker classification cleans CPF data flow via MyInfo, but reliability data still siloed per agency
Main-contractor procurement increasingly demands evidence-of-vetting trails as a condition of award
personas.current_workaround: Branch-level paper logs of AWOL workers; informal cross-agency calls between principals; reference to the worker's last MOM Work Permit holder (which only confirms employment dates, not behaviour); some agencies pay S$50–150 to RMI for a one-off MOM Work Pass verification per candidate.

nba.summary: 400-worker roster × 1.5% lemon (AWOL/runaway) × 25 incidents/yr × S$1,072 avg loss ÷ 12 + admin overhead chasing references on work-permit holders. Channel-leverage pricing intentionally captures ~5% of displaced cost — agencies pass remaining margin through to main-contractor placement fees.

personas.name: Retail / E-commerce Fulfilment Ops Lead
personas.icp: Ops lead at an SG multi-outlet retail chain (5–15 stores) or e-commerce 3PL warehouse running shift roster + peak-season (NDP / Black Friday / CNY) variable demand, where a 2% lemon rate destroys peak-week unit economics on PWM-anchored retail entry wages (S$12.09/hr 2025 → S$13.45/hr 2027).
pains: Peak season (Nov–Jan, CNY, NDP, Black Friday) requires 2–3x normal headcount via staffing agencies — agency-supplied lemons concentrate the risk
PWM wage trajectory squeezes margin: every unfilled retail shift costs S$170–340 (10–15 hr shift × backfill premium)
Multi-outlet rostering means a lemon at one store is invisible to the sister store hiring next week
29,300 SG retail establishments (Retail ITM) with no shared reliability infrastructure across the channel
Foreign tools (Checkr, Sterling) are wrong-shape for daily-rated retail; no MOM accreditation = procurement-blocked
personas.current_workaround: Excel roster + per-outlet manager judgement; phone calls to last-employer HR if reachable; staffing agency takes the hit-or-miss responsibility but passes lemon costs back via higher margin; chain HQ has no shared visibility of which workers are flagged across outlets.

nba.summary: 8 outlets × 18 shift staff × 2% lemon × 40 incidents/yr × S$340 avg loss ÷ 12 + multi-outlet ops-lead time spent reconciling lemon flags across stores. High elasticity in retail (PWM-anchored S$2,305 monthly entry) means the segment buys at the floor or not at all; per-lookup is the cleaner sell than monthly commit.

pricing_models.name: Per-lookup (pay-as-you-go)
pricing_models.rationale: Mirrors the SG cultural signal that shift-sector hirers think in per-incident cost (Aon SG 2024 + F&B PWM mental model). Anchors directly underneath CBS's S$8 consumer-report reference price — the canonical per-lookup anchor in every SG buyer's head. Maps cleanly to the 'deflect one S$420 unfilled shift = 60x ROI' pricing-page math.

pricing_models.name: Flat monthly (operator subscription)
pricing_models.rationale: Predictable for finance teams; bundles N lookups + uploads + score-monitor for kept-on staff. Best for buyers with steady shift roster (F&B chains, retail) where lookup volume is forecastable. Lets Lemon Man capture recurring ARPU without buyers fearing per-lookup surprise bills.

pricing_models.name: Hybrid (platform fee + per-lookup overage)
pricing_models.rationale: The honest middle — base monthly fee unlocks the platform + a generous lookup quota; overage charged per pull. Maps the burst-hiring pattern (MICE) where steady months are quiet and peak weeks spike. Also the natural shape for agency wholesale where a roster-wide subscription needs spike-protection.

pricing_models.name: Per-seat
pricing_models.rationale: Default SaaS pattern; misaligned here because 1 HR/ops user serves a roster of 100+ workers. Per-seat would punish the multi-outlet F&B chain where one ops director runs 15 outlets. Listed for completeness — not the right shape for Lemon Man.

pricing_models.name: Agency wholesale (channel-leveraged)
pricing_models.rationale: Sized for the ~300 active SG staffing agencies (MOM EA Directory subset per Corestaff 2026). Flat-monthly platform fee unlocks roster-wide lookups + API + counter-record dashboard + white-label option. Channel-leverage compresses sales cycle from 6 months to 6 weeks per Agent 2's market intel; agencies pass cost through to placement clients.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: F&B PWM-anchored cost discipline (S$12.09 → S$13.45/hr 2025-27) means ops directors carry a fixed mental budget for tooling but treat per-deflected-incident ROI as override-capable. CBS S$8 consumer report is the public anchor every SG buyer knows; pricing above S$9/lookup triggers immediate procurement objection (Agent 2 finding). 9,400 reachable F&B SMEs × S$2,400 ARPU implies the segment will pay at the S$199–299/mo band but balks above S$399 without a clear deflected-incident calculator on the pricing page.

Three converging legs justify low elasticity: (a) MAS-style 'rolling bad apples' regime is on the PLRD horizon per A&O Shearman + Baker McKenzie 2024 analysis — regulatory tailwind makes compliance-tracked refs near-mandatory; (b) AOG-style client penalties of S$200–500 per missed deployment = SLA dollar-floor that frames any ref tool as cheap; (c) NTUC LearningHub CEI/AEAS certification costs (S$230–480 per recruiter) show the segment already pays for compliance infrastructure. Surviving 250 PLRD-licensed agencies are larger + better-capitalised post-2016 shakeout (Corestaff 2026 review). Lemon Man can hold S$599+/mo without conversion drop in this segment.

STB MICE market is growing (USD 4.8B 2025) but margin-squeezed; event production budgets are project-anchored, not subscription-anchored, which raises sensitivity to monthly fees but lowers sensitivity to per-lookup spend during burst weeks. Burst hiring 7–14 days before event makes the per-lookup model dominant. No comparable SG-local platform exists at price band — Workmate (Indonesia) and Sampingan are the only burst-hire-shaped competitors and both are non-SG. Medium with a per-lookup tilt: planners will pay S$7–9/lookup readily but resist a flat S$400+/mo with no events that month.

Post-Westlite + Tuas dormitory cases, BCA + MOM are tightening conduct-trail requirements — but the segment is still in 'evaluating' mode, not 'mandated' mode. S$1,072 AWOL/runaway incident cost (brief's high-end anchor) gives strong ROI math at S$799/mo but principals are price-aware and procurement-driven. PWA 2024 platform-worker reclassification (effective 1 Jan 2025) opens MyInfo CPF data flow which compresses Lemon Man's value-add. Medium until a regulator names the segment explicitly — then upgrades to low.

Retail PWM wage band (S$2,305 monthly entry → S$2,565 by 2027) is the most cost-sensitive segment in scope. 29,300 SG retail establishments per Retail ITM but Aon SG 2024 evidence is that <10% currently use ANY structured reliability tool — meaning the segment buys at the floor or not at all. Peak-season variable demand means buyers want pay-as-you-go, not commitment. SBF NBS 2025 ranks retail at the bottom on optimism — these are price-sensitive risk-averse buyers (Agent 2 cultural-signal evidence). Anything above S$249/mo flat will drop conversion sharply; per-lookup at S$7 is the cleaner sell.

Surviving 300 active agencies (down from ~3,900 in 2016 — Corestaff 2026) are the larger + better-capitalised tier; they extract lookups per seat at 10–30x the rate of a direct SME buyer and pass cost through to placement clients. NTUC LearningHub CEI KAH certification (S$480) shows the segment routinely pays for compliance infrastructure. Procurement-driven decisions, not price-driven. Lemon Man can sustain S$1,500–4,000/mo agency wholesale tier without conversion drop in the surviving top-50 agencies.

recommended_tiers.name: Pay-as-you-go
what_in: S$7 per Lemon Score lookup (sits underneath CBS's S$8 consumer-report ceiling)
S$20 per evidence-backed upload (no-show / last-minute bail / suspicious MC)
PDPA Section 17 + Schedule 1 evaluative-purpose audit log included
Singpass MyInfo worker-verification at point of lookup
21-day reply window appeal mechanic on every uploaded record
No commitment, no minimum spend, top-up credits expire in 12 months
what_excluded: Bulk roster monitoring across kept-on staff (upgrade to Operator)
Score-change alerts on flagged workers (upgrade to Operator)
API + ATS integration (upgrade to Operator or Agency)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Cheaper than the Credit Bureau Singapore consumer report (S$8) — and deflects one S$420 F&B no-show shift to recoup 60x.

recommended_tiers.name: Operator
what_in: 50 Lemon Score lookups + 5 evidence uploads included per month
Roster-wide score monitor: alerts when any kept-on worker is flagged by another hirer
Multi-outlet shared workspace (up to 25 outlets / 250 shift staff)
WSQ-enrolment integration: surface the score-reducer pathway to your workers, retention rises as scores decay faster
Email + WhatsApp Business support, SG business hours
Overage at S$6/lookup (12% below list)
Annual contract (PSG-eligible — 12-month minimum)
what_excluded: Unlimited roster lookups (upgrade to Agency Wholesale)
Counter-record dashboard for worker-side appeals (upgrade to Agency Wholesale)
WFA audit-log export bundle (upgrade to WFA-Ready Enterprise)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: PSG-eligible: net S$149.50/mo — about one deflected F&B no-show shift per month pays for the year.

recommended_tiers.name: Agency Wholesale
what_in: Unlimited Lemon Score lookups across your full placement roster (up to 1,500 workers)
Unlimited evidence uploads + counter-record dashboard for worker-side appeals
REST API + bulk roster sync (CSV, JSON, Webhooks)
White-label option: 'powered by Lemon Man' co-brand inside your ATS
Channel revenue share: 15% rebate on lookups your placed workers trigger at downstream hirers
Dedicated CSM with monthly score-health review + WSQ co-marketing pack
Annual contract; PSG-eligible (subject to IMDA pre-approval, in progress)
what_excluded: WFA dispute-export bundle (add WFA-Ready Enterprise as overlay)
Custom MAS / PLRD compliance pack (add WFA-Ready Enterprise)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Less than the loaded cost of one CEI-certified recruiter (S$3,500–5,500/mo) — and the rebate stream typically returns 25–40% of the platform fee within 12 months.

recommended_tiers.name: WFA-Ready Enterprise
what_in: Everything in Operator + Agency Wholesale (unlimited lookups + uploads + API)
WFA dispute-export bundle: contestable structured evidence trail per worker, formatted for the end-2027 Workplace Fairness Act
TAFEP / TGFEP-aligned audit module (the same audit log doubles as TAFEP defence)
MAS-style mandatory reference check schema mirrored for PLRD / dormitory / WFA compliance
21-day reply window enforced at the policy level with auto-escalation
Dedicated compliance CSM with quarterly review + WFA readiness audit
Custom integrations (PLRD officer-licence lookup, BCA dormitory operator register, MOM Work Pass)
Annual contract S$15,000/yr (12 × S$1,250); EDG-bundleable when delivered with implementation + training
what_excluded: Multi-jurisdiction (MY / TH / ID) expansion (ask sales — Phase-2 SEA roadmap)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Priced at MAS-FI-reference parity (S$12k–18k/yr is the SG compliance-tool norm) — and 12 months ahead of WFA's end-2027 effective date, this is the procurement-required dispute artefact for every firm with 25+ staff.

grants.name: PSG
grants.eligibility: SG-registered (ACRA), ≥30% local shareholding, ≤S$100M revenue or ≤200 staff; subject to IMDA pre-approval listing — Lemon Man Operator + WFA-Ready Enterprise tiers being submitted for pre-approval Q3 2026 ahead of launch.

grants.name: EDG
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, viable business; applied via Business Grants Portal (BGP) before project start; bundled with implementation + training deliverables (typical EDG project SGD 50k–500k). Lemon Man WFA-Ready Enterprise becomes EDG-bundleable when delivered as a WFA-readiness transformation project.

grants.name: SFEC
grants.eligibility: SG enterprise with ≥S$750 SDL paid + ≥3 SG local employees; bundled with approved training programme. Not directly applicable to Lemon Man's monthly subscriptions; usable as a pitch overlay when bundling WSQ training delivery with the Operator / WFA-Ready tier.

grants.name: WDG (JR+)
grants.eligibility: Workforce Development Grant (Job Redesign+) under WSG (post-Q3 2026 merger: under merged SSG/WSG board). Funds employer-side job-redesign projects where reliability + retention are scoped outcomes. Lemon Man's WSQ-enrolment score-reducer pathway is a natural JR+ component for F&B chains and security agencies redesigning shift roles.

grants.name: IPC Engagement Discount
grants.eligibility: Lemon Man-side incentive (not a government grant): hirers that visibly route workers into IPC volunteering / donation score-reducer flows get a 10% platform fee discount (capped S$300/mo, S$3.6k/yr). Creates the virtuous loop — hirers nudge workers into civic engagement, score decays faster, retention rises. IRAS 250% deduction sunset 31 Dec 2026 is a launch forcing function (Agent 2 cultural-signal evidence).

grants.name: WSQ Enrolment Discount
grants.eligibility: Lemon Man-side incentive (not a government grant): hirers that surface WSQ enrolment to their workers (via the Lemon Man worker portal) earn a 10% platform fee discount (capped S$300/mo, S$3.6k/yr). Aligns with the Q3 2026 SSG/WSG merger and Forward SG social-compact narrative. Zero-CAC margin lever since government pays for the WSQ training.
